item.title: KDE Analysis using spatstat package
item.description:
Getting Started

The code chunk below uses p_load() function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

  • sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
  • tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
  • spatstat package to perform 1st-order spatial point patterns analysis and derive kernel density estimation (KDE) layer.
  • tmap package is used to visualize geospatial data on maps.
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, spatstat, tmap, lubridate)

In the code chunk below, the cleaned data files are loaded into R.

# Load base map
mpsz = st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414)
Reading layer `MPSZ-2019' from data source 
  `C:\magdalenecjw\Capstone\Analysis\data\geospatial' using driver `ESRI Shapefile'
Simple feature collection with 332 features and 6 fields
Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON
Dimension:     XY
Bounding box:  xmin: 103.6057 ymin: 1.158699 xmax: 104.0885 ymax: 1.470775
Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
# Load rds file
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))

Convert Pickup and Dropoff location sf dataframes into spatstat’s ppp object format

Convert the sf dataframe to a ppp object using as.ppp() function from the spatstat package. The result is a marked planar point pattern. To change a marked planar point pattern to just a planar point pattern, simply remove the marks associated with each point using marks(pickup_ppp) <- NULL.

pickup_ppp <- as.ppp(pickup_sf)
Warning in as.ppp.sf(pickup_sf): only first attribute column is used for marks
marks(pickup_ppp) <- NULL

plot(pickup_ppp)

Create observational window (owin)

When analysing spatial point patterns, it is a good practice to confine the analysis with a geographical area like Singapore boundary. In spatstat, an object called owin is specially designed to represent this polygonal region. The code chunk below is used to create a border of Singapore’s land area and convert it into an owin object of spatstat package using as.owin().

# Create border of Singapore's land area
mpsz_border <- st_cast(mpsz %>%
                         summarize(), "POLYGON")

# Convert the resulting sf object to an owin object
mpsz_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_border)

plot(mpsz_owin)

Combine point events object and owin object

In the code chunks below, pick-up and drop-off events located within the observational window are extracted.

pickup_owin = pickup_ppp[mpsz_owin]

plot(pickup_owin)

Pick-up Locations - Edge correction

pickup_owin <- rescale(pickup_owin, 1000, "km")

kde1 <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
kde2 <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)

png(paste0("Pick-up - Edge Correction/pickup_edge.png"), width=1000, height=350)
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(kde1, main="Edge Correction - Default")
plot(kde2, main="Edge Correction - Improved")
dev.off()

Filter by planning area

areas <- unique(mpsz$PLN_AREA_N)

for (i in areas){
  mpsz_filter <- mpsz %>%
  filter(PLN_AREA_N == i)
  
  filter_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_filter)
    
  filter_ppp = pickup_ppp[filter_owin]
  filter_ppp <- rescale(filter_ppp, 1000, "km")
    
  # Initialize an error flag
  error_occurred <- FALSE
  
  # Attempt to calculate kde1
  kde1 <- tryCatch({
    density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error in processing kde1 for area", i, ": ", e$message, "\n")
    error_occurred <<- TRUE  # Update error flag
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  # Skip this iteration if an error occurred in kde1
  if (error_occurred) {
    next
  }
  
  kde2 <- density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)
  
  png(paste0("Pick-up - Edge Correction/pickup_edge_",i,".png"), width=1000, height=500)
  par(mfrow=c(1,2))
  plot(kde1, main="Edge Correction - Default")
  plot(kde2, main="Edge Correction - Improved")
  dev.off()
}

Drop-off Locations - Edge correction

dropoff_ppp <- as.ppp(dropoff_sf)
Warning in as.ppp.sf(dropoff_sf): only first attribute column is used for marks
marks(dropoff_ppp) <- NULL

plot(dropoff_ppp)

dropoff_owin = dropoff_ppp[mpsz_owin]
dropoff_owin <- rescale(dropoff_owin, 1000, "km")

kde1 <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
kde2 <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)

png(paste0("Drop-off - Edge Correction/dropoff_edge.png"), width=1000, height=350)
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(kde1, main="Edge Correction - Default")
plot(kde2, main="Edge Correction - Improved")
dev.off()

Filter by planning area

areas <- unique(mpsz$PLN_AREA_N)

#areas <- "NOVENA"

for (i in areas){
  mpsz_filter <- mpsz %>%
  filter(PLN_AREA_N == i)
  
  filter_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_filter)
    
  filter_ppp = dropoff_ppp[filter_owin]
  filter_ppp <- rescale(filter_ppp, 1000, "km")
    
  # Initialize an error flag
  error_occurred <- FALSE
  
  # Attempt to calculate kde1
  kde1 <- tryCatch({
    density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error in processing kde1 for area", i, ": ", e$message, "\n")
    error_occurred <<- TRUE  # Update error flag
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  # Skip this iteration if an error occurred in kde1
  if (error_occurred) {
    next
  }
  
  kde2 <- density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE, diggle=TRUE)
  
  png(paste0("Drop-off - Edge Correction/dropoff_edge_",i,".png"), width=1000, height=400)
  par(mfrow=c(1,2))
  plot(kde1, main="Edge Correction - Default")
  plot(kde2, main="Edge Correction - Improved")
  dev.off()
}

Pick-up Locations - Bandwidth Selection

bw_diggle <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)

bw_ppl <- density(pickup_owin, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)

bw_abram <- densityAdaptiveKernel(pickup_owin, edge=TRUE)

png(paste0("pickup_bandwidth.png"), width=800, height=500)
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(bw_diggle, main="Fixed Bandwidth - bw.diggle")
plot(bw_ppl, main="Fixed Bandwidth - bw.ppl")
plot(bw_abram, main="Adaptive Bandwidth - bw.abram")
dev.off()

Filter by planning area

areas <- unique(mpsz$REGION_N)

for (i in areas){
  mpsz_filter <- mpsz %>%
  filter(REGION_N == i)
  
  filter_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_filter)
    
  filter_ppp = pickup_ppp[filter_owin]
  filter_ppp <- rescale(filter_ppp, 1000, "km")
    
  # Initialize an error flag
  error_occurred <- FALSE
  
  # Attempt to calculate kde1
  kde1 <- tryCatch({
    density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error in processing kde1 for area", i, ": ", e$message, "\n")
    error_occurred <<- TRUE  # Update error flag
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  # Skip this iteration if an error occurred in kde1
  if (error_occurred) {
    next
  }
  
  kde2 <- density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)
  kde3 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(filter_ppp, edge=TRUE)
  
  png(paste0("Pick-up - Bandwidth/pickup_bw_",i,".png"), width=800, height=700)
  par(mfrow=c(2,2))
  plot(kde1, main="Fixed - bw.diggle")
  plot(kde2, main="Fixed - bw.ppl")
  plot(kde3, main="Adaptive - bw.abram")
  dev.off()
}

Drop-off Locations - Bandwidth Selection

bw_diggle <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)
bw_ppl <- density(dropoff_owin, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)
bw_abram <- densityAdaptiveKernel(dropoff_owin, edge=TRUE)

png(paste0("dropoff_bandwidth.png"), width=800, height=500)
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(bw_diggle, main="Fixed Bandwidth - bw.diggle")
plot(bw_ppl, main="Fixed Bandwidth - bw.ppl")
plot(bw_abram, main="Adaptive Bandwidth - bw.abram")
dev.off()
areas <- unique(mpsz$REGION_N)

#areas <- "NOVENA"

for (i in areas){
  mpsz_filter <- mpsz %>%
  filter(REGION_N == i)
  
  filter_owin <- as.owin(mpsz_filter)
    
  filter_ppp = dropoff_ppp[filter_owin]
  filter_ppp <- rescale(filter_ppp, 1000, "km")
    
  # Initialize an error flag
  error_occurred <- FALSE
  
  # Attempt to calculate kde1
  kde1 <- tryCatch({
    density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.diggle, edge=TRUE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error in processing kde1 for area", i, ": ", e$message, "\n")
    error_occurred <<- TRUE  # Update error flag
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  # Skip this iteration if an error occurred in kde1
  if (error_occurred) {
    next
  }
  
  kde2 <- density(filter_ppp, sigma=bw.ppl, edge=TRUE)
  kde3 <- densityAdaptiveKernel(filter_ppp, edge=TRUE)
  
  png(paste0("Drop-off - Bandwidth/dropoff_bw_",i,".png"), width=800, height=500)
  par(mfrow=c(2,2))
  plot(kde1, main="Fixed - bw.diggle")
  plot(kde2, main="Fixed - bw.ppl")
  plot(kde3, main="Adaptive - bw.abram")
  dev.off()
}

item.title: TKDE Analysis using ks package
item.description:
Getting Started

The code chunk below uses p_load() function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

  • sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
  • tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
  • ks package to perform multivariate kernel density estimation (KDE).
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, ks, lubridate, rgl)

In the code chunk below, the cleaned data files are loaded into R.

# Load base map
mpsz = st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  summarize()

# Load rds file
pickup_loc <- read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_loc.rds") %>%
  mutate(pingtimestamp = as.integer(pingtimestamp)) %>%
  select(-trj_id)
dropoff_loc <- read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_loc.rds") %>%
  mutate(pingtimestamp = as.integer(pingtimestamp)) %>%
  select(-trj_id)
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))

Without normalization

kde_ks_original <- kde(pickup_loc, H = Hlscv(pickup_loc))

summary(kde_ks_original$estimate)

With normalization

# Normalize the data to the range [0, 1]
normalize <- function(x) {
  return ((x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x)))
}

pickup_norm <- as.data.frame(lapply(pickup_loc, normalize))

# Store the original ranges for scaling back
range_x <- range(pickup_loc$pickup_lng)
range_y <- range(pickup_loc$pickup_lng)
range_time <- range(pickup_loc$pingtimestamp)

kde_ks <- kde(pickup_norm, H = Hlscv(pickup_norm))

plot(kde_ks)
